data.title: Microservices
data.description: Amazon uses a microservices architecture to enhance scalability and manageability of its vast e-commerce platform.
data.technical_spec: Decoupled services that handle different functionalities, communicating through APIs.

data.title: AWS Infrastructure
data.description: Utilizes Amazon Web Services for hosting and handling its massive data and computation needs.
data.technical_spec: Cloud-based infrastructure with elastic scaling and global distribution.

data.title: NoSQL Databases
data.description: Incorporation of NoSQL databases like DynamoDB for fast and flexible data retrieval.
data.technical_spec: Distributed database system optimized for high throughput and low latency.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Advanced load balancing approaches are used to evenly distribute incoming network traffic.
data.technical_spec: Multi-tier load balancing with autoscaling support.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Amazon CloudFront is used to deliver content with low latency and high transfer speeds.
data.technical_spec: Global network of edge locations for caching and delivering content.
